description: Small factual corpus — 12 turns of direct identity facts. Baseline test for BM25 + cosine + RRF without multi-hop or temporal complexity.
persona: Sara Chen, software engineer with stable identity facts.

turns.content: Hi! I'm Sara Chen. I just turned 29 this month.
I live in Seattle, Washington. Moved here three years ago for the job.
I'm a senior software engineer at Microsoft. Working on the Azure team.
I have a Shiba Inu named Yuki. She's three years old and loves snow.
I'm vegan — have been for about five years now.
My main hobby is bouldering. I climb at Seattle Bouldering Project two or three times a week.
My partner is Theo. We've been together six years and live together.
I love TypeScript. It's the language I reach for first on every new project.
I'm learning Korean — Theo's family is Korean and I want to talk to his parents in their language.
I prefer remote work. The Microsoft office in Bellevue is fine, but I'm 80% remote.
I drink black coffee — no sugar, no milk. Three cups a day usually.
On Sundays I bake sourdough. Started during the pandemic and never stopped.

probes.query: what programming language does the user love?
what language is the user learning?
what does the user think about cryptocurrency?
